english: tsün-du (tsun du) bgrós-nas (bgros nas) thus declaring their opinions to one another Tar.; to ask advice, či-ltar (ci ltar) bya (bya) źes (zhes) bgrós-nas (bgros nas) asking what they should do Dzl.; to resolve, decide, byá-bar (bya bar) to do Dzl.; bgro-gléṅ (bgro gleng) byéd-pa (byed pa) to dispute, to debate Lex.
1. the walk, gait, mode of walking. - 2. symbol. num.: 2.

to walk, bgród-la (bgrod la) ṗan (phan) this assists in learning to walk Lt.; to go, wander, lam (lam) bgród-pa (bgrod pa) to travel over Glr.; to get through, k̇yód-kyis (khyod kyis) bgród-pai (bgrod pai) skabs (skabs) med (med) ruṅ (rung) although until now you have not been able to get to this place Mil.; ču (chu) bgród-par (bgrod par) dká-ba (dka' ba) a river difficult to cross; nyi-ma-lho-bgród (nyi ma lho bgrod) the sun's going to the south, in the winter half-year, the sun's south declination, byaṅ-bgród (byang bgrod), north declination, bgród-dus (bgrod dus) γnyis (gnyis) S.g. both declinations; bud-méd-la (bud med la) bgród-pa (bgrod pa) to lie with a woman Schr., Cs.
n. of a noted crafty vizier of the king Srontsangampo Glr.

(col. *gár-ra* (gar ra)) smith, mgár-bai (mgar bai) bzo (bzo) smith's work; *gár-zo có-če* (gar bzo co ce) W. to forge; mgár-k̇aṅ (mgar khang), mgár-sa (mgar sa) smithy; γser-mgár (gser mgar) gold-smith Cs.
jaw, jaw-bone, ya-mgál (ya mgal) the upper, ma-mgál (ma mgal) the lower jaw-bone; mgal-čág (mgal chag) a broken jaw-bone, mgal-búd (mgal bud) a dislocated jaw-bone Cs.

also gál-pa (gal pa) a billet of wood; mgal-dúm (mgal dum) 1. a large piece of wood split or cut, 2. a piece of wood half-burnt W., C.; *gal-dó* (gal do), gal tsig* (gal tshig) W., *gal-ró* (gal ro) C. id.; *gal-mé* (gal me) a burning piece of wood, a fire-brand; torch, consisting of long chips or thin billets of wood; mgal-méi (mgal mei) k̇ór-lo (khor lo) a circle of light produced by whirling round a fire-brand.

to rejoice, to be glad, joyful, content; mgú-nas (mgu nas) delighted Mil., Tar.; mgú-bai (mgu bai) lan (lan) ma (ma) byuṅ (byung) he did not receive a gratifying, satisfactory answer Tar. 17, 27; ṫams-čád (thams cad) byin (byin) yaṅ (yang) mgú-dus (mgu dus) med (med) he is never content though every thing be given him Mil.; mgú-bar (mgu bar) byéd-pa (byed pa), W.: *gu čúg-če* (gu cug ce), to exhilarate, to gladden, to make content; dga-mgú-ba (dga' mgu ba), dga-mgu-ráṅ-ba (dga' mgu rang ba) are intensive verbs; myur (myur) = mgú-bar (mgu bar).

resp. 1. throat, neck, gyu (g.yu) mgur-du (mgur du) ṗul-nas (phul nas) presenting (the great teacher) with a turkois for his neck Ma. - 2. voice, mgur snyán-pa (mgur snyan pa) a sweet, harmonious, voice Cs. - 3. (col. *gúr-ma* (gur ma)) song, air, melody, hence a religious song is always designated by the respectful word mgur (mgur) (not by glu (glu)), although the term in itself has no immediate reference to it. mgur (-du) γsúṅ-ba (mgur du gsung ba), bźés-pa (bzhes pa) resp. for glu lén-pa (glu len pa) to sing a song; Sch.: mgur len-pa (mgur len pa) id. - mgur-’búm (mgur 'bum) a hundred thousand Songs, title of the Legends of Milaraspa, which are richly interwoven with songs. - Sch.: mgur bsál-ba (mgur bsal ba) to clear the throat, to hawk, to hem; čú-boi (chu boi) mgur (mgur) 'by-water', a tributary, a subsidiary stream (?).
the god of hunting with the Shamans Sch.

1. neck, throat, mgul-du (mgul du) dógs-pa (dogs pa) to tie, fasten to one's neck e.g. magic objects; ráṅ-gi (rang gi) mgúl-pa (mgul pa) γcód-pa (gcod pa) to cut one's own throat Dzl.; mgúl-pa (mgul pa) nub (nub) his throat is stopped, choked Mng.; mgúl(-pa)-nas (mgul (pa) nas) 'dzin-pa ('dzin pa), *jú-ba* (ju ba), to seize by the throat, sometimes also used for mgúl-pa-nas (mgul pa nas) k̇yúd-pa ('khyud pa) to fall on a person's neck, to embrace. - mgúl-nad (mgul nad) disease of the throat, sore throat. - mgul-číṅs (mgul chings) dkár-po (dkar po) a white neck-cloth Pth. - mgul-dár (mgul dar) or dpa-dár (dpa' dar) a silk cloth tied round the neck as a badge of honour. - 2. the shoulder of a mountain Mil., γyón-mgul-na (g.yon mgul na) on the left slope.

resp. dbu (dbu) 1. head, *gó-la zug rag* (go la zug rag) I have a headache, a pain in my head W.; *mgo k̇or* (mgo khor) my head turns, I feel dizzy, I am getting confused, perplexed; mgo skór-ba (mgo skor ba) to cheat, swindle, deceive; mi-mgo ma skor (mi mgo ma skor) do not cheat <
